claim: Preflight sequencing: inject schema context into LLM tools only at moment of invocation, not upfront, to keep context window clean and avoid overwhelming the agent with all possible contexts
actionable: When building agent tools that access databases/APIs, defer loading schema/context until tool is called; pass context at runtime, not in system prompt
source_title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business?

claim: Layering rules: tell LLM to distrust its own knowledge about internal systems and prioritize primary sources (docs, databases) over training data
actionable: In system prompt, explicitly instruct agents: 'Do not rely on your training knowledge about our products; always verify against primary sources (database schemas, doc files)'
source_title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business?

claim: Query validation: always run generated database queries and validate they return data before hardcoding into reusable widgets; catches 'valid SQL, zero rows' failure mode
actionable: For any LLM-generated query (SQL, etc.), execute it, check row count > 0, validate results look reasonable before deploying to users; don't assume syntactic correctness = semantic correctness
source_title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business?

claim: Reusable widgets are declarative JavaScript that calls data sources directly; once generated, subsequent runs are deterministic and cheap (no LLM cost per execution)
actionable: Have agents generate widget code once; widgets call APIs/DB directly; store widgets for reuse; users can refresh data without re-invoking LLM
source_title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business?

claim: Embedding schema context and join patterns (e.g., '4-join-deep customer-to-user relationship') once in the guidance layer teaches agents the pattern for all future queries
actionable: Document complex schema relationships, join patterns, and filter rules in context/guidance; agents learn these once and reuse them; reduces errors in subsequent queries
source_title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business?

claim: For complex data estates with unclear entity relationships, self-descriptive column names and status columns ('active', 'deleted') help LLM query correctly without extensive context
actionable: When designing database schemas for LLM agents, use clear column names, include status columns, document foreign key relationships; this enables agents to generate correct queries with minimal context
source_title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business?

claim: Self-serve BI reduces bottleneck where non-technical staff ask data questions: instead of waiting for engineers, they ask agent, which builds query or interactive dashboard themselves
actionable: Deploy agent-powered BI tools (Studio pattern) for support teams, product teams, marketing; enable self-serve query generation; engineers focus on schema/guidance, not query one-offs
source_title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business?
